title: fraxinus oxycarpa var. oligophylla
intro: Fraxinus oxycarpa var. oligophylla, also known as the Manchurian ash or Chinese ash, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, characterized by its broad, lobed leaves and dark gray bark. The bark of Fraxinus oxycarpa var. oligophylla has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and it is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain. Herbal preparations of the bark have been used to treat a range of conditions, including gastrointestinal issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. The bark can be dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
